title: Added tutorial on how to include Buildah as library

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
bot, add author to whitelist

@slinkydeveloper thanks for the PR but it looks like our CI doesn't like some white spaces at the end of a line or two...

```
** ERROR: dangling whitespace found in these files: **
docs/tutorials/04-include-in-your-build-tool.md:97:``` 
** ERROR: try running "sed -i -E -e 's,[[:space:]]+$,,'" on the affected files **
Makefile:73: recipe for target 'validate' failed
make: *** [validate] Error 1
```
@slinkydeveloper looks really good over all.  Once complete, would you consider creating a blog on buildah.io?

@TomSweeneyRedHat of course, what should i write on it? An extended version of this tutorial? Can you point me where should i write it?

Anyway, I think it would be wise to publish the godocs of buildah together with this tutorial :smile:

@slinkydeveloper buildah.io uses *.md format for the blog posts.  Once this is complete, I'd use this tutorial as the starting point and perhaps add a bit more introductory verbiage.  buildah.io runs from a GitHub repository, so you'd just need to do the standard clone, add, commit, push loop to that repo.

The repo lives at:  https://github.com/containers/buildah.io  and the README.md has a small list of formatting/setting up stuff that you'll need to do.  The main ones are you'll need to add your info to `_config.yml` and then have the headers that are listed on that page in your blog post.

If you've any trouble with any of that, feel free to ping me.  If you find the directions in the README.md aren't clear, please let me know so we can clean them up.

Thanks!

@slinkydeveloper Thanks, might be worth writing this up as a blog and talking about how you are using it.

@rh-atomic-bot r+

@slinkydeveloper I'm seeing a strange error the homu tests:

```
$ make vendor
0.27s$ ./hack/tree_status.sh
tree is dirty, please commit all changes and sync the vendor.conf
```

Can you `git fetch`, `git rebase` and push again please?  @rhatdan other thoughts?
Rebased and stashed, hope it works :smile:
LGTM and happy green test buttons!  Thanks @slinkydeveloper !
